title: Anatomy of a Confidence Interval
description: A visual breakdown of the components and calculation process for a confidence interval, showing how sample data leads to an interval estimate for a population parameter.
elements: Margin of Error (MOE = Critical Value * SE)
relationships: Sample Data is used to calculate the Point Estimate and Standard Error
Standard Error and Critical Value (based on chosen confidence level, e.g., 95%) are multiplied to get the Margin of Error
Point Estimate plus/minus Margin of Error defines the Confidence Interval
The Confidence Interval provides a range believed to contain the true Population Parameter
